stage: USB C4E controlled WebUI two-profile lifecycle test
sequence: start FOXAI WebUI locally without browser
legacy GET starts Safe Normal CPU
verify safe profile has no whitelist
explicit POST starts Approved Custom Nodes CPU
request Safe Normal CPU while approved profile is healthy

r1_repair.root_cause: The test client timed out after 3 seconds while the live WebUI /api/status route was still generating a valid local status response.
changes: allow 20 seconds for one full GET /api/status response
extend WebUI readiness deadline from 90 to 150 seconds

r2_repair.root_cause: C4E-R1 retained only 200,000 bytes of a 794,671-byte served WebUI page for contract searching, so the later profile POST route text was not searched.
changes: capture up to 2,000,000 bytes for the exact served-page contract check
require proof that the current served page was captured completely
preserve all R1 readiness and lifecycle controls

r3_repair.root_cause: R2 validated Windows' platform subprocess audit representation rather than the original structured Python Popen request.
changes: guard the actual structured Popen argv before platform conversion
allow only four exact lifecycle-controller actions
deny shell, executable override, wrong cwd/root, unknown action, and unguarded Popen
retain raw audit-event arguments for evidence

r4_repair.root_cause: The final evidence parser searched the platform Popen event for the R2 decision label instead of pairing the R3 high-level guard record with its authorized platform audit record.
changes: require one-to-one pairing and no unguarded platform events
require only the four approved lifecycle-controller action shapes
